title: Softmax rowwise int8 r8 acc24 retryproof block
hypothesis: A fresh standalone rowwise int8 softmax r8 acc24 block under a unique canonical path can validate submission recovery after evaluator-side auth or network failure without losing the original reviewable runs diff.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one fresh bounded softmax block run prove that completion-time frozen evidence preserves submission retryability after submission interruption?
include: one standalone int8 rowwise softmax r8 acc24 block under a unique design path
one forced submission interruption followed by submission retry
exclude: parameter broad sweep beyond the single retryproof candidate
follow_on_broad_sweep: none unless the recovery path still loses canonical review evidence

expected_benefit: proves that evaluator-side submission retries survive later pulls and repo updates
adds one more bounded circuit_block physical reference for the softmax rowwise family
risks: this is a workflow-proof item more than a product-improvement item
the block may be physically redundant relative to the existing acc24 reference

expected_result.reason: the first goal is to complete one fresh bounded run and prove that submission retry uses frozen completion-time evidence
